ism_description: Operating system and application patches are applied within 48 hours for critical vulnerabilities, and within one month for non-critical vulnerabilities on EC2 instances and Azure VMs.
notes: AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager and Azure Update Manager automate patching. Customer responsible for patch baseline configuration and scheduling.

ism_description: Host-based intrusion detection systems are implemented on all EC2 instances and Azure VMs to detect unauthorised access attempts and malicious activity.
notes: Amazon Inspector provides vulnerability scanning; GuardDuty provides threat detection. Full HIDS requires customer-deployed agents (e.g., CrowdStrike, Wazuh).

ism_description: Data at rest on compute instances is encrypted using AES-256 or stronger encryption. Data in transit between compute instances uses TLS 1.2 or higher.
notes: EBS encryption with KMS and Azure Disk Encryption with Key Vault provide encryption at rest. TLS enforcement is customer responsibility.

ism_description: Privileged access to compute instances is controlled via just-in-time access, multi-factor authentication, and audit logging of all privileged sessions.
notes: SSM Session Manager and Azure Bastion eliminate the need for SSH/RDP exposure. JIT access via Defender for Cloud. MFA enforced via IAM/Entra ID policies.

ism_description: Compute instances use Trusted Platform Module (TPM) and Secure Boot capabilities to verify instance integrity at launch and prevent tampering.
notes: AWS Nitro provides hardware-enforced security. Azure Trusted Launch enables Secure Boot and vTPM. Customer must select appropriate instance types and enable features.

ism_description: Serverless function code is scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment, and runtime environments are isolated from other workloads.
notes: Lambda functions run in isolated execution environments. Azure Functions use sandbox isolation. Inspector and Defender provide code vulnerability scanning.

ism_description: Container images are scanned for known vulnerabilities (CVEs) using automated scanning tools before being pushed to a registry and deployed to production.
notes: ECR enhanced scanning with Inspector and ACR integration with Defender for Containers provide automated CVE scanning. Customer must configure scan-on-push policies.

ism_description: Kubernetes Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) is configured to enforce least-privilege access, with namespace isolation between workloads and teams.
aws_services: AWS IAM Roles for Service Accounts
notes: EKS and AKS support native Kubernetes RBAC. Customer must define and enforce RBAC policies, namespace quotas, and network policies. IRSA and Azure Workload Identity for pod-level access.

ism_description: Runtime threat detection is enabled for container workloads to detect anomalous process execution, network connections, and privilege escalation attempts.
notes: GuardDuty for EKS/ECS provides runtime threat detection. Defender for Containers provides runtime protection for AKS. Alerts integrated with Security Hub and Sentinel.

ism_description: Container workloads access secrets and credentials via a dedicated secrets management service, with secrets never stored in container images or environment variables.
notes: AWS Secrets Manager CSI driver and Azure Key Vault CSI provider enable secret injection. Customer responsible for rotation policies and access scoping. No native enforcement of secret exclusion from images.

ism_description: Network policies are enforced between Kubernetes pods to restrict east-west traffic to only permitted communication paths between services.
notes: VPC CNI and Azure CNI support Kubernetes NetworkPolicy resources. App Mesh and Azure Service Mesh provide mTLS and traffic policy. Customer must define and apply network policies.

ism_description: Container images are immutable after build. Image signing is used to verify image integrity and provenance before deployment to production clusters.
notes: ECR supports image signing via Notary v2 and AWS Signer. ACR supports content trust. However, admission control enforcement (e.g., Kyverno, OPA Gatekeeper) must be deployed by the customer — not provided natively.

ism_description: Access to container registries is restricted to authorised users and service accounts. Pull-through cache and cross-account access are controlled via resource-based policies.
notes: ECR resource-based policies and ACR RBAC roles (AcrPull, AcrPush) provide granular access control. AWS Organizations SCPs can restrict cross-account ECR access.

ism_description: Fargate and Azure Container Apps workloads run with read-only root filesystems and drop all unnecessary Linux capabilities to minimise attack surface.
notes: Fargate blocks privilege escalation and limits capabilities by default. Customer must configure securityContext in pod specs. Azure Container Apps provide managed isolation but require customer hardening.

ism_description: Access to AI/ML API endpoints, including Amazon Bedrock and Azure OpenAI Service, is restricted to authorised identities using IAM policies and API keys with least-privilege scopes.
notes: Bedrock enforces IAM-based access control. Azure OpenAI uses Entra ID RBAC. Private endpoints restrict network access. Customer responsible for key rotation and scope management.

ism_description: All AI model inference requests, including prompts and model responses, are logged with sufficient detail to support forensic investigation and compliance auditing.
notes: CloudTrail logs Bedrock API calls. Azure Monitor logs OpenAI requests. Full prompt/response content logging requires customer configuration of model invocation logging to S3 or Log Analytics workspace.

ism_description: AI training data and model artefacts are stored within Australian data centre regions to comply with Australian data sovereignty requirements under the Privacy Act 1988.
notes: AWS ap-southeast-2 (Sydney) and Azure australiaeast/australiasoutheast regions available. Bedrock limited model availability in AU. Customer must configure regional endpoints and data residency policies.

ism_description: AI model outputs are filtered through content safety mechanisms to detect and block harmful, biased, or policy-violating responses before delivery to end users.
notes: Bedrock Guardrails provide content filtering. Azure AI Content Safety provides harm detection. Customer configures content filtering policies aligned to organisational acceptable use.

ism_description: AI model integrity is verified through checksums and provenance attestation. Data poisoning prevention controls are implemented for training pipelines.
notes: No native data poisoning detection in Bedrock or Azure OpenAI. SageMaker ML Lineage Tracking and Azure ML model registry provide provenance. Customer responsible for training data validation pipelines.

ism_description: Third-party AI foundation models accessed via Bedrock Marketplace or Azure AI Foundry are assessed for security and compliance before use in production systems.
iso_control_name: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
notes: AWS and Azure conduct supplier assessments for model providers. Customer must perform risk assessments for each third-party model and document approvals per IRAP and ASD guidance.

ism_description: AI systems implement controls to detect and mitigate prompt injection attacks, adversarial inputs, and jailbreak attempts that could cause the model to bypass safety controls.
iso_control_name: Security testing in development and acceptance
notes: No native prompt injection detection in Bedrock or Azure OpenAI. Bedrock Guardrails provide partial protection. Customer must implement input validation, prompt hardening, and adversarial testing.

ism_description: Multi-factor authentication is required for all privileged users accessing AWS and Azure management consoles, APIs, and identity providers.
notes: AWS IAM MFA enforcement via SCPs and Entra ID Conditional Access enforce MFA. IAM Identity Center supports hardware tokens and authenticator apps.

ism_description: All API calls, management console actions, and configuration changes are logged to tamper-evident audit trails retained for a minimum of seven years.
notes: CloudTrail with S3 Object Lock (WORM) and Azure Monitor with immutable storage provide tamper-evident logging. Customer configures retention periods and log integrity validation.

ism_description: Threat detection and anomaly detection services are enabled across cloud accounts to identify indicators of compromise, misconfigured resources, and insider threats.
notes: GuardDuty provides ML-based threat detection. Security Hub aggregates findings. Defender for Cloud and Sentinel provide SIEM/SOAR capabilities. Customer configures alert thresholds and response playbooks.

ism_description: Web application firewalls are deployed in front of internet-facing applications to protect against OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities and DDoS attacks.
notes: AWS WAF with managed rule groups and Azure WAF with OWASP rulesets provide layer 7 protection. AWS Shield Advanced and Azure DDoS Protection provide volumetric DDoS mitigation.

ism_description: Secrets, API keys, database credentials, and certificates are stored in a dedicated secrets management service with automated rotation and access auditing.
notes: Secrets Manager with automatic rotation and Key Vault with soft-delete and purge protection provide comprehensive secrets management. Customer must migrate all secrets from code/config files.

ism_description: Security incident response procedures are documented and tested, with automated alerting to the Security Operations Centre for critical and high-severity findings.
notes: Security Hub EventBridge rules and Sentinel playbooks automate alerting. Incident Manager provides runbook automation. Customer must develop and test incident response playbooks.

ism_description: Access to cloud storage is restricted to authorised identities using least-privilege bucket policies, private ACLs, and no public access enabled by default.
notes: S3 Block Public Access and Azure Blob public access disabled by default. Bucket policies and RBAC roles enforce least-privilege. AWS Organizations SCPs prevent public bucket creation.

ism_description: All data stored in cloud object storage is encrypted at rest using customer-managed encryption keys stored in a dedicated key management service.
notes: S3 SSE-KMS with customer-managed keys and Azure Blob encryption with CMK in Key Vault. AWS Config rule checks for S3 encryption. Customer manages key rotation schedules.

ism_description: Cloud network infrastructure is segmented using virtual private clouds and subnets, with traffic between segments controlled by security groups and network ACLs.
notes: VPC with security groups/NACLs and Azure VNet with NSGs provide network segmentation. PrivateLink and Private Link enable private connectivity to services without internet exposure.

ism_description: Cloud resource configurations are continuously assessed against security baselines using infrastructure-as-code scanning and configuration compliance services.
notes: AWS Config rules and Security Hub standards (CIS, PCI-DSS, NIST) provide continuous compliance. Azure Policy and Defender for Cloud secure score track resource compliance.

ism_description: Cloud administrator accounts are assigned based on business need with documented access reviews conducted quarterly. Accounts are disabled immediately upon staff departure.
notes: Access reviews supported by IAM Access Analyzer and Entra ID Access Reviews. Automated deprovisioning requires integration with HR systems. Customer responsible for off-boarding procedures.

ism_description: Cloud service provider data centres used to process or store Australian Government data meet physical security requirements equivalent to Australian Government facility standards.
notes: AWS and Azure data centres are ISO 27001, SOC 2, and IRAP assessed. Compliance reports available via AWS Artifact and Azure Trust Center. Provider maintains physical security.

ism_description: Auto Scaling Groups and Azure VM Scale Sets are configured with hardened, pre-approved machine images that have been scanned for vulnerabilities before use.
notes: EC2 Image Builder automates golden AMI creation with CIS benchmarks. Azure Compute Gallery distributes hardened VM images. Inspector and Defender scan images before deployment.

ism_description: All administrative actions within Kubernetes clusters are logged to a centralised SIEM, including API server audit logs, pod creation events, and RBAC changes.
notes: EKS control plane logging to CloudWatch and AKS diagnostic logging to Log Analytics. Customer must enable all log categories and configure log retention and alerting.

ism_description: All network traffic between cloud services uses private connectivity endpoints, avoiding traversal of the public internet for sensitive data flows.
notes: PrivateLink and Private Link provide private access to managed services. Direct Connect and ExpressRoute provide dedicated private connectivity from on-premises. Customer configures DNS resolution for private endpoints.

ism_description: Service accounts and workload identities used by applications are assigned only the minimum permissions required and are not shared between different workloads.
aws_services: AWS IAM Roles for Service Accounts
notes: IRSA for EKS pods and Azure Managed Identity provide workload-level identity without shared credentials. IAM Access Analyzer validates least-privilege. Customer responsible for role design.

ism_description: SageMaker training jobs and Azure ML experiments are monitored for anomalous resource usage, unexpected data access, and deviations from expected training behaviour.
notes: CloudWatch monitors SageMaker metrics. GuardDuty detects anomalous API calls. Azure Monitor tracks ML experiment metrics. Full behavioural monitoring of training jobs requires custom instrumentation.

ism_description: Infrastructure is provisioned using approved Infrastructure-as-Code templates that have been security scanned and reviewed before deployment.
notes: CloudFormation Guard and Terraform Sentinel policies enforce security requirements. Azure Policy as Code prevents non-compliant deployments. Customer maintains IaC repositories and review processes.

ism_description: Audit logs from all cloud services are centralised in a dedicated logging account or workspace, protected from deletion, and retained for at least seven years for Australian Government systems.
notes: CloudTrail Organisation Trail centralises logs to a dedicated S3 bucket with Object Lock. Azure Monitor diagnostic settings centralise logs in Log Analytics. Customer configures retention and access controls.

ism_description: Zero-trust network access principles are applied to workloads, requiring all traffic to be authenticated and authorised regardless of network location.
notes: AWS Verified Access and Azure AD Application Proxy provide zero-trust application access. VPC security groups and NSGs enforce microsegmentation. Customer must define and enforce access policies.

ism_description: Encryption keys used to protect Australian Government data are managed by the organisation and are not accessible to the cloud service provider.
notes: AWS CloudHSM and Azure Dedicated HSM provide FIPS 140-2 Level 3 key management. Customer-managed keys in KMS/Key Vault ensure provider cannot access plaintext data.

ism_description: Cloud environment incident response capabilities include automated remediation for common security events, with escalation paths to human responders for complex incidents.
notes: Security Hub custom actions and Sentinel SOAR playbooks automate responses. Customer must develop playbooks, test them, and maintain an up-to-date incident response plan.

ism_description: Azure Kubernetes Service and Amazon EKS clusters have audit logging enabled for all API server requests, authentication events, and authorisation decisions.
notes: EKS audit logging to CloudWatch Logs Insights. AKS audit logs to Log Analytics. All log categories (api, audit, authenticator, controllerManager, scheduler) must be explicitly enabled.

ism_description: AI/ML models processing sensitive data are deployed within private network boundaries with no public internet exposure, using private endpoints and VPC/VNet integration.
notes: SageMaker VPC mode and Bedrock private endpoints restrict traffic to VPC. Azure ML VNet integration and OpenAI Private Link prevent public exposure. Customer must configure DNS and routing.

ism_description: Container orchestration platforms are deployed with pod security admission controllers to enforce security standards and prevent deployment of non-compliant workloads.
notes: EKS supports Pod Security Standards (PSS). AKS supports Azure Policy for Kubernetes (Gatekeeper). Customer must configure and enforce appropriate security profiles (Restricted, Baseline, Privileged).

ism_description: Cloud resource configurations are continuously monitored for drift from approved security baselines, with automated alerting and remediation for non-compliant resources.
notes: AWS Config conformance packs and Azure Policy initiatives enforce security baselines. Config remediation actions and Azure Policy remediation tasks auto-fix non-compliant resources.

ism_description: AI model access is governed by an approved model registry that documents each model's risk classification, intended use cases, and applicable data handling restrictions.
notes: SageMaker Model Registry and Azure ML model registry provide technical catalogues. Risk classification and governance documentation are customer responsibilities with no native enforcement.

ism_description: TLS 1.2 or higher is enforced for all communications between services. Older TLS versions and weak cipher suites are explicitly disabled in load balancer and API gateway configurations.
notes: ALB and CloudFront SSL policies enforce minimum TLS versions. Azure Front Door and Application Gateway enforce TLS 1.2+ by default. Customer must configure SSL policies on all endpoints.

ism_description: Privileged Identity Management is implemented for cloud administration accounts, requiring approval workflows and time-limited activation for administrative roles.
notes: Azure PIM provides JIT role activation with approvals. AWS IAM supports permission boundaries. No native JIT access in IAM — requires third-party tools or custom automation. Customer responsible for PIM configuration.

ism_description: Cognitive services and AI APIs are monitored for unusual call volumes, unauthorised access attempts, and patterns indicating API abuse or credential compromise.
notes: CloudWatch metrics for Rekognition/Comprehend and Azure Monitor for Cognitive Services provide usage monitoring. Anomaly detection requires customer-configured alerts and thresholds.

ism_description: ECS task definitions and Azure Container Instances are reviewed and approved before deployment. Container CPU and memory limits are set to prevent resource exhaustion.
notes: ECS task definition revisions enable change tracking. Resource limits enforced via task definition CPU/memory constraints. Azure ACI resource limits set at deployment. Customer manages definition reviews.

ism_description: Differential privacy and data anonymisation techniques are applied to training datasets containing personal information to prevent re-identification of individuals from model outputs.
iso_control_name: Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information
notes: No native differential privacy in SageMaker or Azure ML for standard training. SageMaker supports custom privacy-preserving techniques. Microsoft Research tools available but not integrated. Customer must implement.

ism_description: Break-glass emergency access procedures exist for cloud environments with dedicated emergency access accounts that have all activity logged and are subject to post-use review.
notes: Azure Entra ID break-glass accounts documented in Microsoft guidance. AWS requires customer-designed emergency access procedures. Both require customer implementation and testing.

ism_description: Lambda function and Azure Function configurations are managed through Infrastructure-as-Code with version control. Function code is reviewed and approved before deployment.
notes: Lambda versioning and aliases enable controlled deployments. Azure Functions deployment slots provide staged rollouts. IaC with Terraform or Bicep manages function configurations.

ism_description: CloudTrail and Azure Activity Log events are analysed in real-time using SIEM tools to detect security incidents and policy violations, with alerts sent to the SOC within 15 minutes.
notes: CloudTrail to Security Hub EventBridge rules and Sentinel analytics rules enable real-time detection. Customer configures alert thresholds and SOC notification procedures.

ism_description: Application performance and availability are monitored continuously with dashboards and automated alerts for service degradation, errors, and availability issues.
notes: CloudWatch Container Insights and Azure Application Insights provide application observability. Customer configures dashboards, alarm thresholds, and notification channels.

ism_description: Red team exercises are conducted quarterly against AI systems to identify prompt injection vulnerabilities, model bypasses, and data leakage risks before they are exploited.
iso_control_name: Security testing in development and acceptance
notes: No native AI red teaming in AWS or Azure. Microsoft PyRIT framework available as open source. AWS provides penetration testing guidance. Customer must establish and fund AI red team programme.

ism_description: Mutual TLS (mTLS) authentication is used for service-to-service communication within containerised and microservices environments.
notes: App Mesh and Istio on EKS support mTLS with SPIFFE/SVID certificates. Azure Service Mesh (Istio) on AKS provides mTLS. Customer must configure mTLS policies and certificate rotation.

ism_description: Unnecessary system services, protocols, ports, and features are disabled on all compute instances and container hosts to minimise the attack surface.
notes: CIS-hardened AMIs and Azure Marketplace CIS images provide baseline hardening. AWS Systems Manager Run Command and Azure VM extensions automate hardening scripts. Customer must verify and maintain configurations.

ism_description: Cloud storage data classification policies are implemented to identify, label, and apply appropriate protection controls to sensitive and official data stored in S3 and Azure Blob.
notes: Amazon Macie discovers and classifies sensitive data in S3. Microsoft Purview provides data classification and labelling across Azure storage. Customer configures classification policies aligned to ISM data classifications.

ism_description: Azure Kubernetes Service clusters and Amazon EKS use encrypted etcd storage to protect Kubernetes secrets at rest within the control plane.
notes: EKS encrypts etcd with KMS envelope encryption for Kubernetes Secrets. AKS encrypts etcd with platform-managed keys by default, with CMK support via Key Vault. Customer must enable KMS encryption on EKS.

ism_description: Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) is generated for all container images and AI model dependencies to enable rapid identification of affected components during supply chain incidents.
iso_control_name: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
notes: Amazon Inspector generates SBOM for container images. Defender for Containers provides vulnerability reports. Formal SBOM generation with Syft/CycloneDX and policy enforcement are customer responsibilities.

ism_description: AWS Config and Azure Policy compliance reports are reviewed weekly by the cloud security team to identify and remediate configuration drift and policy violations.
notes: AWS Audit Manager automates evidence collection for IRAP assessments. Azure Policy compliance dashboard provides centralised view. Customer must schedule and document review processes.

ism_description: AI inference APIs exposed to users are rate-limited and throttled to prevent abuse, denial of service, and excessive cost from malicious or automated requests.
notes: Bedrock model invocation throttling and Azure OpenAI quotas and rate limits are configurable. API Gateway and APIM provide additional rate limiting and usage plans. Customer configures appropriate limits.

ism_description: Azure App Service and AWS Lambda functions are deployed in dedicated virtual network environments to prevent lateral movement and enforce network isolation.
notes: Lambda VPC integration and App Service VNet integration enable private connectivity. Customer must configure subnet assignments, security groups, and NSGs for isolation.

ism_description: Azure Container Registry and Amazon ECR are monitored for unauthorised image push/pull events, and alerts are generated for any images pushed outside of approved CI/CD pipelines.
notes: CloudTrail logs ECR API calls. GuardDuty detects anomalous ECR access patterns. Azure Monitor and Defender for Containers alert on suspicious registry activity. Customer configures alert rules.

ism_description: Australian Government data is only processed and stored in cloud regions that have undergone IRAP assessment at the applicable security classification level.
notes: AWS ap-southeast-2 and Azure Australia regions are IRAP assessed for PROTECTED. AWS GovCloud equivalents not available in Australia. Customer must use SCP/Policy to restrict deployments to approved regions.

ism_description: VPC and VNet flow logs are enabled and analysed to detect anomalous network traffic patterns, data exfiltration attempts, and lateral movement within the cloud environment.
notes: VPC Flow Logs and NSG Flow Logs provide network traffic visibility. GuardDuty analyses flow logs for threats. Traffic Analytics and Sentinel UEBA correlate network events. Customer enables and retains flow logs.

ism_description: AI-generated content is watermarked or labelled to ensure recipients can identify content produced by AI systems, supporting transparency and provenance requirements.
notes: No native AI content watermarking in Bedrock or Azure OpenAI. Azure AI Content Safety provides some provenance features. Customer must implement metadata tagging and content labelling at application layer.
